judgment.summary: Dominant, high-growth semiconductor manufacturer with strong margins and a solid balance sheet, trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 4.7%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 35.1% YoY and Earnings growth 58.4% YoY indicate robust top and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin 61.9%, Operating margin 58.1%, and Profit margin 46.5% demonstrate exceptional profitability.
Forward P/E of 20.58x is attractive given the high growth rates and market leadership.
Strong balance sheet with $3.38T cash and $1.09T debt, and a high Free Cash Flow of $719.16B.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $467.84, suggesting significant upside potential.
negatives: Price/Book of 61.43x suggests a high valuation relative to book value.
Beta of 1.26 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.
Dividend yield of 0.9% is modest, which may not appeal to income-focused investors.

news.paragraph: TSMC is benefiting from the ongoing AI rally, with recent headlines highlighting its inclusion in "best semiconductor stocks" discussions and a Buy rating reiterated by BofA. The company also saw a positive development in a trade secrets case where its unit will not appeal a ruling. While broader market news mentions Dow futures falling, Asian shares are surging, indicating a generally positive sentiment for tech-exposed equities.

headlines.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall After Strong Nvidia Earnings; SpaceX Files For Huge IPO
Asian shares surge after oil prices slip and Wall Street resumes its AI rally
Tokyo Electron's Taiwan unit says it will not appeal ruling in TSMC trade secrets case
Is Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) One of the Best Semiconductor Stocks With the Highest Upside Potential Right Now?
BofA Reiterates Buy on Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) After Tech Symposium

examples.body: $TSM just sold. Concerned about Taiwan invasion.
